actionable_feedback: Embed the official vendor-observed_risk_score formula directly in the workbook (named range or dedicated 'scoring' sheet) and remove the 'needs_review' temporary stand-in. If the vendor formula cannot be embedded, add a single-cell provenance link to the vendor source and document the exact substitution with justification.

Make the MW vs MWh dispatch-window assumption explicit in the spreadsheet: add a unit-conversion column (MWh->MW or vice versa) with the dispatch-window hours used, and an automated flag that highlights any record where treating MW and MWh equivalently would change classification.

Add row-level, cell-level citations that show for each record: source packet values, the formula cell(s) that computed observed_risk_score, the margin computation, and the final class. Use named ranges and cell comments where possible so reviewers can trace calculations without re-deriving them externally.

Include a compact 'verification' sheet that reproduces the final_decision_table and contains a one-row-per-record CSV-export-friendly summary (record_id, base_risk, selected_risk, margin, class, citation_cell_refs). This enables graders to validate numbers even if openpyxl cannot be used in their environment.

Persist a reproducible sensitivity-run control: implement +/-5% toggle cells and a small table that auto-populates classification outcomes for the sensitivity scenarios; update the memo to reference those cells.

Ensure the workbook is saved without macros, with plain formulas and named ranges, and produce a short plain-text 'verification_summary.txt' that repeats the key numeric results and cell references to aid automated previews.
artifact_specific_comments: dispatch_memo.md correctly documents the assumption and flags needs_review; update it after embedding the vendor formula and add precise cell citations.

unit_commitment_repair.xlsx contains formula cells (workbook_contains_formula_cells=270) and the final_decision_table, but add a verification sheet and cell-level provenance to increase grader confidence and eliminate the need_review note.
failed_or_weak_rubric_items: Observed_risk_score formula was not packet-sourced and is marked needs_review
MW vs MWh treated as one dispatch-window equivalent without embedded conversion checks
failed_rubric_items: Observed_risk_score formula was not packet-sourced and is marked needs_review
MW vs MWh treated as one dispatch-window equivalent without embedded conversion checks
feedback_summary: Good deliverable: required artifacts present, internal calculations and portfolio selection are consistent, sensitivity analysis performed and expert-review flags used properly. Score reduced to 0.85 because the workbook relies on an assumed observed_risk_score formula (documented as needs_review) and treats MW and MWh as equivalent without embedded unit-conversion checks. Those documented ambiguities prevent full semantic confidence. Also note that the preview environment could not parse the xlsx (openpyxl unavailable), so additional embedded verification (CSV/summary) will improve graders' ability to re-evaluate.
